channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan Businessman's Four-Year Journey to Homeownership and Car Ownership
item.description: Kyagera Peterson, a Kenyan businessman, shared his journey of building a successful business from scratch, leading to owning a house and car. Starting in 2012 with just determination, he grew his ente...

item.title: JRU edges San Beda in thrilling preseason clash
item.description: The Jawi-Rizal University (JRU) basketball team made a strong preseason statement by defeating San Beda University in a closely contested match. Key players FilOil Mangubat and Hubilla emerged as stan...

item.title: Rating of Performers in the 2026 World Cup Final Halftime Show

item.title: Somizi Mhlongo addresses criticism over TikTok video inspired by Nonhle Thema's interview
item.description: Somizi Mhlongo has publicly addressed the controversy surrounding his viral TikTok video, which some followers claimed was linked to media personality Nonhle Thema’s recent radio interview. The entert...

item.title: Nadra Employee Missing After Leaving Karachi Office, Family Reports
item.description: Fayyaz Ahmed, an employee of the National Database and Registration Authority (Nadra) and son of senior journalist Sohail Sangi, has gone missing since leaving his office in Karachi on Monday evening....

item.title: Two Sadhus Found Dead in Forest Near Haridwar Temple; Police Investigation Ongoing
item.description: Two sadhus, Sewa Giri and Uttam Giri, were discovered buried in a pit within a nearby forest after disappearing since Monday. The Pathri police station initiated a search operation led by Rabindra Sin...

item.title: Attorney General Nominee Faces Sanctions for Epstein Document Non-Compliance
item.description: Todd Blanche, President Donald Trump's nominee for U.S. Attorney General, is facing potential sanctions after a federal judge ruled he violated the Epstein Files Transparency Act. The law mandates the...

item.title: Sugar Production Resumes, Easing Supply and Price Concerns in Tanzania
item.description: Local sugar producers in Tanzania are gradually resuming operations after months of disruptions caused by heavy rains, maintenance delays, and technical challenges. This has led to improved availabili...

item.title: FRSC Conducts Special Patrol to Address Overloading and Road Safety in North-West
item.description: The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) has started a three-day special patrol in Sokoto, Kebbi, and Zamfara states to tackle overloading, mixed loading, and other traffic violations linked to road crash...

item.title: Travis Scott Engages in Bottle-Throwing Incident at Exclusive New York Club
item.description: American rapper Travis Scott found himself in a chaotic situation during a private event at New York's Zero Bond club. The incident occurred when Scott believed another guest was filming him, leading ...

item.description: The article explores the heightened security measures surrounding Pauline Hanson's political events, highlighting the intense scrutiny and logistical challenges of hosting large gatherings. It delves ...

item.description: Police and military forces have completed their security preparations for the first-ever parliamentary elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), which are scheduled on ...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Ransom Money Not Required as Evidence in Kidnapping Cases
item.description: The Supreme Court (SC) ruled that actual ransom money does not need to be presented in court to secure a conviction for kidnapping for ransom. In a decision penned by Associate Justice Ricardo Rosario...

item.title: KSE-100 edges higher amid global energy market volatility
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange's (PSX) benchmark index, the KSE-100, recorded marginal gains on Tuesday as investor sentiment remained cautious due to uncertainties in global energy markets. The index op...

item.title: Reader Letters and Humorous Musings from Column 8
item.description: The article features a column titled 'Column 8' from The Age, showcasing reader-submitted letters that blend humor with cultural references. Contributors share anecdotes about travel experiences, such...

item.title: Small Businesses in Calgary Concerned Over Potential Milk Shortages Following Plant Incident
item.description: The article discusses growing concerns among small businesses in Calgary regarding potential milk shortages linked to an electrical issue at a Saputo plant. The incident, which occurred on July 20, 20...

item.title: ADNOC's Strategic Expansion Across Africa's Energy Markets
item.description: The Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) is intensifying its efforts to establish a significant presence across multiple African countries through strategic acquisitions and partnerships. This move ...

item.title: Former Warri Refinery MD charged with money laundering, gets N500m bail
item.description: A former Managing Director of the Warri Refining and Petrochemical Company Limited, Jimoh Yisawu, was arraigned before the Federal High Court in Abuja on Monday over alleged money laundering charges. ...

item.title: Congolese salon owner faces xenophobic confrontation over business ownership
item.description: A Congolese entrepreneur in Melmoth, South Africa, destroyed her own salon equipment after being confronted by a group claiming foreign nationals cannot own salons. The woman, who is legally residing ...

item.title: ICPC probes Chief of Staff over alleged fake agency scandal
